summary_one_line: Input must build a valid SQL token sequence reaching a specific yy_reduce grammar reduction; value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient incrementally solves the keyword/token comparisons.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A byte sequence that parses into the specific SQL grammar production whose reduce action is dispatched at yy_reduce:157966 — i.e. real SQL keyword tokens (e.g. 'vacuum', ';') laid out so the LALR parser reaches that reduce rule, rather than malformed/garbage token streams that fault out earlier.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Both winner-resolving seeds carry literal SQL keyword bytes: Seed 1 begins ';vacuum\n\n' and Seed 2 begins 'vacuum' followed by quote/semicolon punctuation, so they tokenize into a recognized statement that drives the parser to the true side of L157966 (W: T=2 F=17).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short, malformed token soup ('SELEEC .`', '""/"" ', 'T 1; c BINARY...') that never tokenize into the production reaching the reduce action — the false side dominates (L: T=0 F=131) so the true branch is never taken across naive/cmplog runs.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds CMP_MAP gradient feedback: each integer/string CMP records a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket, so a partial keyword match (e.g. progressively matching 'vacuum') is rewarded with a new coverage bucket and preserved as corpus. This lets value_profile / value_profile_cmplog climb toward the exact token strings the grammar needs, whereas naive and cmplog (lacking CMP_MAP) get no gradient on these keyword comparisons and stall.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs differ by exactly the value_profile technique, with value_profile-bearing fuzzers winning.
The second decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog over cmplog, again with delta value_profile.
The blocker is inside the LALR parser reduce-action dispatcher yy_reduce.
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side while the loser never does.
A winner-resolving seed begins with the literal SQL keyword 'vacuum' preceded by a semicolon.
A second winner-resolving seed begins with the literal SQL keyword 'vacuum'.
Loser-blocking seeds are short malformed token streams, e.g. a corrupted SELECT and bare quote punctuation.
value_profile's distance bucket is a coarse encoding of how close two CMP operands were, rewarding partial keyword matches with new coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The distance bucket is a coarse encoding of how close the two
operands were (Hamming distance bucket for `trace_cmp*`; matching-prefix
length for string/memory CMPs).

evidence_trail.claim: An input producing a CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any seen before adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved, giving the gradient that drives the winner.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory lineage check does not apply. The technique attribution rests on both decisive pairs having delta=value_profile (value_profile beats naive; value_profile_cmplog beats cmplog), isolating value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback as the divergence cause; no dash-row lineage verification is required for this mechanism.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same yy_reduce-style keyword-token gate where value_profile shows no advantage over naive (equal resolve rates), indicating the CMP_MAP gradient is not what lets the winner assemble the required SQL token sequence.

weakest_evidence_point: The mapping from the keyword bytes in the winning seeds to the specific grammar production reduced at L157966 is inferred from yy_reduce's role plus the T=2/T=0 split, not from a confirmed parse trace of those exact seeds.
